content: Just as human language learners use lexical and written reflection (the linguistic sense of reflexion) to refine grammatical accuracy and communicative clarity, large language models can integrate human-like metacognitive reflection routines from second language learning into their self-reflection mechanisms to improve output quality. This means LLM self-reflection could be framed not just as an AI technical correction step, but as a structured learning practice analogous to how human students revise their work through deliberate reflexion exercises.
